language.title: Interface Language
language.description: Choose the language for the app interface

free.title: All Features Unlocked
free.description: This is a free, open-source build. All features are fully available at no cost.
free.noLicense: No license key required. No subscription. No data sent to any server.

momorApi.title: Free & Open Source
momorApi.description: Configure your own AI provider API keys in the AI Providers tab. No subscription or Momor account required.
momorApi.noAccount: All processing happens locally using your own keys.

general.mousePassthroughDesc: Overlay stays visible but lets all mouse clicks pass through to the app beneath.
general.openOnLogin: Open Momor when you log in
general.openOnLoginDesc: Momor will open automatically when you log in to your computer
general.disguiseDesc: Disguise Momor as another application to prevent detection during screen sharing.
general.themeDesc: Customize how Momor looks on your device
general.overlayOpacityDesc: Adjust the transparency of the assistant overlay.
general.opacityDesc: Controls the visibility of the in-meeting overlay.
general.retentionDesc: When enabled, live assistance works but transcripts, summaries, and history are discarded when the meeting ends
general.verboseLoggingDesc: Print detailed audio, STT, and pipeline diagnostics
general.versionText: You are currently using Momor version {{version}}
general.transcriptDesc: Show real-time transcription of the interviewer
general.autoScrollDesc: Automatically scroll to the latest message as new responses arrive
general.sectionDesc: Customize how Momor works for you
general.aiLanguageDesc: Language for AI suggestions and notes
general.cloudScopesDesc: Disable a data type to block it from cloud LLM providers. Local providers are unaffected.
general.screenUnderstandingDesc: How Momor reads what is on your screen.
general.visionFirstDesc: Try every configured vision provider; first success wins.
general.visionOnlyDesc: Require a vision-capable provider; never silently drop screenshots.
general.privateVisionDesc: Use a local vision model only. Never call cloud vision.
general.technicalInterviewVisionDesc: Highest-resolution image profile so code stays sharp.
general.disguiseUndetectableHint: Disable Undetectable mode to change disguise.

keybinds.title: Keyboard shortcuts
keybinds.desc: Momor works with these easy to remember commands.

audio.speechProviderDesc: Choose the engine that transcribes audio to text.
audio.sttProfilesDesc: Speech-to-text: add providers, set a default, switch from the overlay during meetings.
audio.sttNoProfiles: No STT profiles yet. Add a provider above.
audio.sttAddDialogDesc: Pick a transcription engine to configure.
audio.sttAllAdded: All available providers are already in your list.
audio.sttAlreadyInList: This provider is already in the list — scroll to the highlighted card.
audio.sttEmptyHint: No providers yet. Add Deepgram, Groq, local Whisper, or another.
audio.sttRestartRequired: Restart the app (stop the terminal and run npm run electron:dev again) to load updates.
audio.localWhisperDesc: On-device transcription with no cloud audio upload.

calendar.title: Visible Calendars
calendar.desc: Upcoming meetings are synchronized from these calendars
calendar.connectPrompt: Get started by connecting a Google account.

overlay.noProvider: No AI provider configured. Add an API key in Settings.
overlay.noSttProvider: No STT provider selected. Open Settings → Audio to pick one.
overlay.askAnything: Ask anything on screen or conversation, or
overlay.stealthHotkeyConflict: Stealth typing hotkey {{key}} is already in use. Click the input to activate, or rebind in Settings.
overlay.stealthPermissionMissing: Stealth typing needs Accessibility access. Grant it in System Settings, then restart Momor.
overlay.sttOpenAudioSettings: Open Audio settings to select a provider

userContext.title: AI context & profiles
userContext.subtitle: Tell the AI who you are, what you're doing now, and how it should respond.
userContext.footerHint: Changes save automatically. Pick the active profile during a call from the overlay.
userContext.personalHint: Background, experience, highlights — anything that helps the AI represent you better.
userContext.personalPlaceholder: E.g. I'm a software engineer with 5 years in backend, focused on Go and distributed systems. I want to sound confident but honest about gaps...
userContext.sessionHint: Interview, meeting, code review, presentation — describe today's specific scenario.
userContext.sessionPlaceholder: E.g. Technical interview for a senior backend role at Company X. Focus on system design and leadership...

sessionPresets.interview: Job interview. I need clear, professional answers I can speak out loud.
sessionPresets.meeting: Work meeting. Keep answers concise and aligned with the team and project context.
sessionPresets.codeReview: Code review. Prioritize technical clarity, trade-offs, and practical improvement suggestions.
sessionPresets.presentation: Presentation or demo. Answers should be concise, confident, and easy to verbalize.
sessionPresets.sales: Sales or negotiation call. Consultative tone, value focus, and objection handling.

userContext.profilesListeningSectionHint: How the AI behaves while you're in a meeting.
userContext.behaviorHint: Tone, format, and constraints for AI replies.
userContext.behaviorPlaceholder:
E.g. Reply only in code blocks. No prose explanations.

Or: Talk to me like a senior mentor. Max 3 sentences.
userContext.activeListeningHint: Keeps context of what you say, but only auto-reacts to PC audio.
userContext.autoSuggestHint: Generates suggestions when it detects questions on PC audio.

common.enterApiKeyFirst: Please enter an API key first

launcher.title: My Momor
launcher.errorElectronApi: Error: Electron API not initialized. Check preload script.

meetingChat.errorNoResponse: Couldn't get a response. Please try again.
meetingChat.errorCheckSettings: Couldn't get a response. Please check your settings.
meetingChat.errorGeneric: Something went wrong. Please try again.
meetingChat.errorInvalidApiKey: The active model's API key is invalid. Check Integrations and your selected model.
meetingChat.errorNoProvider: No AI provider configured. Add an API key in Settings → Integrations.

providers.defaultModelDesc: Primary model for new chats. Other configured models act as fallbacks.
providers.activeModelsDesc: Choose the default model for chat and for vision/images. They can match or differ.
providers.fastResponseModeDesc: Super fast responses using the Codex CLI fast model, Groq, or Momor. Turn this off to use the selected normal model.
providers.fastResponseRequires: Requires Groq or Codex CLI to be configured.
providers.fastResponseAlert: Please configure Groq or Codex CLI first to enable Fast Response Mode.
providers.integrationsDesc: Configure AI providers and speech transcription in one place.
providers.addIntegrationDesc: Choose what to connect. It only appears in the list after you add it.
providers.allIntegrationsAdded: All available integrations have been added.
providers.configureProviderFirst: Add a provider below to choose the active model.
providers.keySavedPlaceholder: Key already saved — enter a new one to replace
providers.cloudProvidersDesc: Add API keys to unlock cloud AI models.

backupKeys.title: Backup keys
backupKeys.hint: Used automatically when the primary key fails (limit, quota, auth error).

providers.codexCliDesc: Route text and screenshot responses through a locally authenticated Codex CLI.
providers.enableCodexCliDesc: Adds Codex CLI as a selectable local backend and fallback.
providers.ollamaNoModels: Ollama is running but no models found. Run `ollama pull llama3` to get started.
providers.customProvidersDesc: Add your own AI endpoints via cURL.
providers.responsePathDesc: Dot notation path to the answer text in the JSON response. If empty, the full JSON is returned.
providers.openClaudeDesc: Anthropic Claude via OAuth browser login — no API key required
providers.codexCliOAuthDesc: Codex (ChatGPT) via OAuth terminal login — no API key required
providers.oauthCliHint: Sign in with OAuth in the browser or terminal. No API key paste needed when the CLI is logged in.
providers.claudeApiOptional: Optional: API key below. For Claude Code without a key, add the «Claude Code (OpenClaude)» integration.
providers.integrationsListDesc: Every provider uses the same layout. Add only what you need.
providers.removeKeyConfirm: Are you sure you want to remove the {{provider}} API key?
providers.deleteProviderConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this provider?

about.title: About Momor
about.subtitle: Designed to be invisible, intelligent, and trusted.

phoneLink.title: Phone Link Integration
phoneLink.desc: Connect your iOS or Android device to seamlessly use it as a wireless remote microphone or companion screen for your meeting notes.

smartTask.title: Smart Task Sync
smartTask.desc: Action items are now auto-extracted with extreme precision and can be instantly exported to Jira, Linear, or Asana boards.

speakerId.title: Speaker Identification
speakerId.desc: Advanced real-time speaker diarization automatically tags individual speakers by name throughout the meeting transcript.

offlineMode.title: Expanded Offline Mode
offlineMode.desc: Now featuring 100% offline transcription and intelligent note generation using specialized, lightning-fast on-device SLMs.

hybridIntelligence.title: Hybrid Intelligence
hybridIntelligence.desc: Seamlessly routes queries between ultra-fast models for instant speed and reasoning models (Gemini, OpenAI, Claude) for complex tasks. Powered by enterprise-grade speech recognition from 7+ providers.

localRag.title: Local RAG & Memory
localRag.desc: A purely local vector memory system allows Momor to recall details from past meetings. Embeddings and retrieval happen on-device via SQLite for maximum privacy.

stealthControl.title: Stealth & Control
stealthControl.desc: Features "Undetectable Mode" to hide from the dock and "Masquerading" to disguise as system apps. You control exactly what data leaves your device.

noRecording.title: No Recording
noRecording.desc: Momor listens only when active. It does not record video, take arbitrary screenshots without command, or perform background surveillance.

featureSpotlight.slide1Line1: I built all of this thinking of you — line by line, with care.
featureSpotlight.slide1Line2: This app is my gift, and you're the reason behind the name.
featureSpotlight.slide2Subtitle: Use it on calls, technical interviews, and important meetings.
featureSpotlight.slide2Bullet1: I'll be quietly cheering for you every time
featureSpotlight.slide2Bullet2: Discreet answers + summaries when you're done
featureSpotlight.slide3Line1: If something's missing today, don't worry.
featureSpotlight.slide3Line2: Lots of loving updates are on the way, just for you.
featureSpotlight.slide4Line1: Thank you for being my inspiration.
featureSpotlight.slide4Line2: Now go show them what you're made of — I believe in you.

whisper.localEngineDesc: Select the AI models you want to use for Speech-to-Text inference.
whisper.splitChannelsDesc: Use different models for microphone and system audio
whisper.whisperxServerDesc: Connect to a self-hosted WhisperX server that exposes an OpenAI-compatible transcription endpoint.
whisper.enableWhisperxServerDesc: Use the local server for transcription instead of bundled models
whisper.limitedHardware: Limited hardware — cloud STT recommended for long sessions

quota.resetsOn: Resets on your next billing date

app.incompatibleWarning: {{count}} meetings used your previous AI provider ({{oldProvider}}) and won't appear in search results under {{newProvider}}.

startup.subtitle: The ultimate AI meeting assistant
startup.agreement: By clicking Continue, you agree to our

update.checkConnection: Check your internet connection or download the update manually from GitHub.
update.downloadStarted: The download has started in your browser. Follow these steps to install the update:
update.clearQuarantine: 1. Clear quarantine on the downloaded file:
update.openInstall: 2. Open the file and install Momor.
update.clearQuarantineInstalled: 3. Clear quarantine on the installed app:
update.pleaseWait: Please wait while we prepare the update.
update.moveToApplications: Move app to Applications folder, then run:
update.performanceImprovements: Includes performance improvements and bug fixes.

help.title: Help & Setup Guide
help.subtitle: Learn how to deeply configure Momor. Everything from providing the right API scopes to executing conversational interviews seamlessly is covered below.
help.skipSetup: Want to skip the manual setup?
help.useMomorApi: Use the Momor API for an out-of-the-box experience. One-click zero-configuration usage.
help.quickStartSubtitle: Get Momor running in four steps.

permissions.title: Grant Permissions
permissions.desc: Enable Screen Recording and Accessibility for Momor in macOS Privacy & Security.

audio.title: Set Up Audio
audio.desc: Open Settings → Integrations, pick a speech provider (local Whisper, Deepgram, or Google), and add any required API keys.

aiModel.title: Connect an AI Model
aiModel.desc: Open Settings → Integrations, add a provider (Gemini, Groq, OpenAI, Claude, or local CLI), and set your active model.

personalize.title: Personalize (Optional)
personalize.desc: Tune keybinds, overlay opacity, and speech providers in Settings to match your workflow.

configureIntegrations.title: Configure integrations
configureIntegrations.desc: Add API keys and connect speech providers in Integrations.

permissions.intro: Momor runs on-device but needs OS permissions for screen context and global shortcuts. Your system should look like this:
permissions.micDesc: Pick your input device and system output capture in Integrations → Speech. By default Momor follows the OS default route.
permissions.sckDesc: Recommended on macOS 13+ for low-latency speaker loopback.
permissions.coreAudioDesc: Fallback for older hardware. Use only if SCK drops packets.
permissions.languageDesc: Set spoken language and regional accent (e.g. en-US vs en-GB) so STT matches your inflection.
permissions.screenDesc: Lets Momor read the screen when you capture context.
permissions.screenPath: System Settings → Privacy & Security → Screen Recording
permissions.a11yDesc: Required for global shortcuts regardless of focused window.

audioStt.intro: Momor supports multiple speech engines. Open Settings → Integrations, add a speech profile, and set it as default.
audioStt.apiDesc: Test connections before a live meeting. You will see success pings or explicit errors.

interfaceOps.intro: When running, Momor stays as a lightweight overlay — your command center during calls.

launcherHome.title: Launcher & session history
launcherHome.intro: The home window lists past sessions grouped by day (Today, Yesterday, or a date). Each row shows the title, duration pill, and start time on the right.
launcherHome.times: Times follow your language: 24-hour in Portuguese (e.g. 15:44), 12-hour in English. While a session is processing, the row shows a spinner instead of the clock.
launcherHome.refresh: Use the refresh control in the header to reload the list from local storage — no cloud calendar sync.
launcherHome.searchHeader: The search pill in the header filters sessions by title and opens the same global palette as Cmd+K.

meetingIntel.intro: When a session ends, Momor saves a local dossier: transcript, token usage, and structured summaries.
meetingIntel.summaryDesc: A background job compresses the transcript into markdown with overview and action items.
meetingIntel.transcriptsDesc: Full dialogue timeline with speaker labels and timestamps.
meetingIntel.storageDesc: See tokens used for visual and text inputs during the session.
meetingIntel.semanticDesc: Use the ask bar at the bottom of meeting details instead of re-reading the whole transcript.
meetingIntel.contextualDesc: Ask short questions; Momor injects the relevant transcript slice.
meetingIntel.memoryDesc: Chat here stays scoped to this meeting only.

globalSearch.intro: Press Cmd+K (or Ctrl+K on Windows) anywhere to open the global palette — or use the search pill on the Launcher header.
globalSearch.meetingTraversalDesc: Typing fuzzy-matches titles and summaries. Enter opens that session.
globalSearch.fallbackDesc: If nothing matches, start a new LLM chat with your query.
globalSearch.shortcutsDesc: Work system-wide even when Momor is hidden. Change them in Settings → Hotkeys.
globalSearch.showHideDesc: Toggle overlay visibility — quick panic hide.
globalSearch.screenshotDesc: Silent capture fed into the LLM context flow.
